intent: A centralized inventory management platform designed to synchronize stock levels across multi-site warehouse nodes. The core value proposition is the 'Auto-Replenishment Engine,' which monitors SKU thresholds in real-time. The workflow includes supplier-integrated database entries, automated triggers for low-stock alerts when inventory drops below 10 units, and dynamic purchase order generation sent directly to linked vendor APIs.

entities.name: WarehouseNode
entities.description: Represents a physical storage facility with a unique identifier, geolocation data, and current operational status for synchronization.

entities.name: StockKeepingUnit
entities.description: The master record for individual products, containing global attributes, supplier mapping, and the universal reorder threshold setting.

entities.name: InventoryLedger
entities.description: An immutable audit trail entity that records every increment or decrement event linked to a specific WarehouseNode and StockKeepingUnit.

entities.name: ReplenishmentTrigger
entities.description: A state machine entity that tracks the lifecycle of low-stock events, moving from 'Detected' to 'PO_Generated' and 'Vendor_Confirmed'.

entities.name: PurchaseOrderRequest
entities.description: A generated entity capturing the payload for external vendor APIs, containing quantity, supplier ID, and delivery destination details.

entities.name: VendorIntegrationPoint
entities.description: Stores endpoint configurations, authentication tokens, and API schema mapping required to communicate with external supplier replenishment systems.

entities.name: ThresholdSnapshot
entities.description: A read-optimized cached view of current stock levels across all WarehouseNodes, updated via asynchronous stream processing to support real-time monitoring.

architecture.description: The system utilizes an event-driven architecture where every stock movement is captured in the InventoryLedger and published to a message bus. A stream processor updates the ThresholdSnapshot in real-time. Upon a decrement event, the logic triggers the ReplenishmentTrigger service if the balance falls below the StockKeepingUnit threshold. The ReplenishmentTrigger service queries the VendorIntegrationPoint to fetch the correct endpoint and constructs a PurchaseOrderRequest. This request is transmitted asynchronously to the vendor via an API gateway, with status updates transitioning the ReplenishmentTrigger through its state machine until delivery is confirmed. This decoupling ensures high availability and scalability across distributed warehouse sites.

endpoints.summary: Configure a new vendor integration point

rules.description: Ensure InventoryLedger entries remain immutable and sequential.
rules.logic: Every ledger record must contain a cryptographic hash or sequence number linking to the previous record; updates to existing records are strictly prohibited.
If (CurrentBalance < StockKeepingUnit.ReorderThreshold) and (ReplenishmentTrigger state is null or Terminal), create a new ReplenishmentTrigger with status 'Detected'.
Transition ReplenishmentTrigger from 'Detected' to 'PO_Generated' only if valid VendorIntegrationPoint exists for the target StockKeepingUnit supplier.
Disable stock increment or decrement operations for any WarehouseNode where operational_status != 'Active'.
If ThresholdSnapshot indicates a balance disparity greater than 0% relative to the sum of InventoryLedger movements, trigger a reconciliation event.
Move ReplenishmentTrigger to 'Vendor_Confirmed' and finalize associated PurchaseOrderRequest upon receipt of successful asynchronous webhook acknowledgment from VendorIntegrationPoint.
